---
title: "DESeq2 multiple conditions"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*.

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.

# Initial setup

```{r Rmd setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/project/def-mouellet/Scripts_MOU/PNP/alliancecan/deseq2/")
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r Set working directory}
# Set working directory
setwd("/project/def-mouellet/Scripts_MOU/PNP/alliancecan/deseq2/")
getwd()
```

```{r Activate renv}
# Activate environment
renv::activate()
```


```{r Load libraries}
# Load required packages
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggrepel)

# Set seed
set.seed(42)
```





# Load data

```{r Count data}
# Count data
data <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/input/Mov10_full_counts.txt", 
                   header = T, row.names = 1)
str(data)

# If count data is not integer, round to integer
# data <- data %>%
#   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ as.integer(round(.))))
```

```{r Col data}
# Col data / Sample info / Metadata
meta <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/input/Mov10_full_meta.txt", 
                   header = T, row.names = 1)

# Convert to factor
meta[] <- lapply(meta, as.factor) # all columns
str(meta)
```

```{r Sample info}
meta
```

```{r Check sample name match}
# Check that sample names match in both files
## Composition check
all(colnames(data) %in% rownames(meta)) # Should be TRUE
## Order check
all(colnames(data) == rownames(meta))
## If order check gives FALSE
data_raw <- data
data <- data_raw[, match(rownames(meta), colnames(data_raw))]
all(colnames(data) == rownames(meta))
```





# QC

## Count distribution

```{r Count data long format}
# Long format
data_long <- data %>%
  gather(
    colnames(data),
    key = "sample_name",
    value = "raw_counts"
  )
```

```{r Count histogram}
# Count distribution
ggplot(
  data_long,
  aes(x = raw_counts)
  ) +
  geom_histogram(stat = "bin", bins = 100) +
  facet_wrap(~ sample_name) +
  xlab("Raw expression counts") +
  ylab("Number of genes") +
  theme_bw()
```

A low number of counts associated with a large proportion of genes



## Count data modeling

Poisson: mean == variance

Binomial: mean \< variance

```{r Mean - variance plot}
# MOV10_overexpression replicates
mean_counts <- apply(data[, c("Mov10_oe_1", "Mov10_oe_2", "Mov10_oe_3")], 1, mean)
var_counts <- apply(data[, c("Mov10_oe_1", "Mov10_oe_2", "Mov10_oe_3")], 1, var)
df <- data.frame(mean_counts, var_counts)

ggplot(df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = mean_counts, y = var_counts)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = mean_counts, y = mean_counts, color="red"), show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_classic()
```



## Normalized counts

```{r DESeqDataSet object (QC)}
# Create DESeqDataSet object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = data,
  colData = meta,
  design = ~sampletype
)
```

```{r Original count matrix}
# The original count matrix
head(counts(dds))
```

```{r DESeq2 normalization}
# DESeq2 normalization: median of ratios
dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
sizeFactors(dds)
```

```{r Normalized counts matrix}
# Normalized counts matrix
normalized_counts <- counts(dds, normalized = TRUE)
write.table(normalized_counts, 
           file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts.tsv", 
           sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```



## Normalized counts transformed
Only necessary for visualization in QC

### rlog transformation
rlog() works well on small datasets (n < 30)

```{r rlog()}
rld <- rlog(dds, blind = TRUE)
rld_mat <- assay(rld)
write.table(rld_mat, 
           file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_rlog.tsv", 
           sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

### vst transformation
vst() is faster than rlog(), recommended for medium to large datasets (n > 30)

```{r vst()}
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE) # n genes >= 1000
# vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind = TRUE) # n genes < 1000
vsd_mat <- assay(vsd)
write.table(vsd_mat, 
           file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_vst.tsv", 
           sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

### log2(n+1) transformation

```{r log2(n+1)}
ntd <- normTransform(dds)
ntd_mat <- assay(ntd)
write.table(ntd_mat, 
            file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_log2.tsv", 
            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```



## PCA
This example use rlog transformation. vst or log2(n+1) could be used similarly

### PC1 & PC2

```{r plotPCA()}
plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "sampletype") # Top 500 most variable genes
# plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "sampletype", ntop = 100) # Top 100 most variable genes
```

```{r PCA data}
pcaData <- plotPCA(rld, intgroup = c("sampletype", "MOVexpr"), returnData = TRUE)
pcaData
```

```{r percentVar}
# Get the percentage variation
attributes(pcaData)
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pcaData, "percentVar"))
```

```{r PCA ggplot2}
# Plot PCA using ggplot2
ggplot(
  pcaData,
  aes(
    x = PC1, y = PC2,
    color = sampletype, 
    shape = MOVexpr
    )
) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percentVar[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percentVar[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_classic()

ggsave("example_multiple_conditions/output/PCA.pdf", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

### Additional PCs

```{r PCs data}
pca <- prcomp(t(rld_mat)) # all genes
attributes(pca)
```

```{r PCs dataframe}
as.data.frame(pca$x)
```

```{r PCs dataframe with metadata}
pca_w_meta_df <- merge(
  as.data.frame(pca$x), 
  meta, 
  by = 0, # row names as merging key
  all = TRUE # full join
  )
pca_w_meta_df
```

```{r Percentage variation}
# Percentage variation
percentVar <- round((pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100, 1)
percentVar
```


```{r PC3-4 ggplot2}
ggplot(
  pca_w_meta_df,
  aes(
    x = PC3, y = PC4,
    color = sampletype, 
    shape = MOVexpr
    )
) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC3 (", percentVar[3], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC4 (", percentVar[4], "%)")
    ) +
  theme_classic()
```



## Hierarchical Clustering
This example use rlog transformation. vst or log2(n+1) could be used similarly

```{r rlog data}
# Load rlog data
rld_mat <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_rlog.tsv", 
                      header = T, row.names = 1)
head(rld_mat)
```

### Correlation matrix

```{r Pairwise correlation}
# Pairwise correlation
rld_cor <- cor(rld_mat)
rld_cor
```

```{r Correlation heatmap}
# Correlation heatmap, default
pheatmap(rld_cor)
```

```{r Customized heatmap}
# Correlation heatmap, customized
heat.colors <- brewer.pal(9, "Blues")
pheatmap(
  rld_cor, 
  color = heat.colors,
  cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
  border_color=NA, fontsize = 10, height=20
)
```

```{r Save heatmap}
# Save heatmap
heat.colors <- brewer.pal(9, "Blues")
png("example_multiple_conditions/output/correlation_heatmap.png", width = 8, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
pheatmap(
  rld_cor, 
  color = heat.colors,
  cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
  border_color=NA, fontsize = 10, height=20
)
dev.off()
```



### Count matrix

```{r Normalized count data}
# Load normalized data
normalized_counts <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts.tsv",
                                header = T, row.names = 1)
head(normalized_counts)
```

#### Genes highly expressed

```{r Top expressed genes}
# Top 20 most expressed genes
top20_expression_genes <- order(rowMeans(as.matrix(normalized_counts)), decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]

normalized_counts[top20_expression_genes,] %>%
  row.names()
```

```{r Top expressed gene heatmap}
# Heatmap
pheatmap(
  rld_mat[top20_expression_genes, ],
  cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 10,
  annotation_col = meta
)
```

#### Genes highly varied

```{r Top varied genes}
# Top 20 genes with most variance
top20_variance_genes <- order(rowVars(as.matrix(normalized_counts)), decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]

normalized_counts[top20_variance_genes,] %>%
  row.names()
```

```{r Top varied gene heatmap}
# Heatmap
pheatmap(
  rld_mat[top20_variance_genes, ],
  cluster_cols = TRUE, cluster_rows = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 10,
  annotation_col = meta
)
```





# Differential expression analysis

```{r DESeqDataSet object (DEA)}
# Create DESeqDataSet object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = data,
  colData = meta,
  design = ~ sampletype
)
```

## Pre-filter (optional)
https://support.bioconductor.org/p/65256/

```{r Pre-filtering}
# Option 1: Filter genes where there are less than 10 counts
# idx <- rowSums(counts(dds)) >= 10
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 2: Filter genes where there are less than 3 samples having at least 5 counts
# idx <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 5) >= 3
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 3: Filter genes where there are less than 3 samples with normalized counts greater than or equal to 5
# dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
# idx <- rowSums(counts(dds, normalized=TRUE) >= 5 ) >= 3
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 4: Only keep genes that have a total count of at least 10 in any condition
# cond <- colData(dds)$condition
# idx <- rep(FALSE, nrow(dds))
# for (c in levels(cond)) {
#   idx <- idx | rowSums(counts(dds)[, cond == c, drop=FALSE]) >= 10
# }
# dds <- dds[idx,]

# Option 5: Only keep genes that have at least 10 count in every sample of at least 1 condition
# cond <- colData(dds)$condition
# idx <- rep(FALSE, nrow(dds))
# for (c in levels(cond)) {
#   mat <- counts(dds)[, cond == c, drop = FALSE]
#   idx <- idx | rowSums(mat >= 10) == ncol(mat)
# }
# dds <- dds[idx,]
```



## Run DESeq2

```{r Run DESeq2}
# Run the DE analysis
dds <- DESeq(dds)
```

Dispersion (α): Var = μ + α*μ^2

Dispersion ~ 1/μ, ~ Var

```{r Dispersion estimates}
# Dispersion estimates
plotDispEsts(dds)
```



```{r}
# Draft
```
